title: What I think about the Olympics - Request

transcript.text: Request video from Martin R. Cruz, who is 80% Filipino and 20% Mexican.
Hello, Kyle. How are you? Next video request.
Do you like to watch the Olympics every time it comes on?
If so, can you please talk about your thoughts on the Olympics in general and why?
Who are some of your favorite athletes? Also, are you looking forward to the next?
Oh, wow. There's even more. To the next. Winter Olympics. 2014, Sochi, Russia.
In the summer, 2016, Olympics. Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Thank you and have a nice day.
Do I like to watch the Olympics every time it comes on? Yeah, dude. I love watching the Olympics.
In fact, when the Olympics come on, I try to watch everything.
I try to be a religious watcher and I put everything below the Olympics. I'll watch anything.
So, if so, can you please talk about your thoughts on the Olympics in general and why?
Well, my thoughts on the Olympics in general, well, I could talk about the history of the Olympics.
The Olympics started in about 1350 BC. Yeah, long ass time ago.
And it's from the town called Olympia.
And it was a neutral town in the middle of the Peloponnesian Peninsula in Greece.
And all of the different city-states would kind of donate money to keep it maintained.
And then every four years, they would send all of their, you know, best athletes over there.
You know, the rich people who could afford to do the training and buy the horses and all that kind of stuff.
And the original games were wrestling.
I think they did it naked and oiled up.
And then sprinting and chariot racing was a really big one.
The sprint was about like 50 meters or something because I was at Olympia and I saw the actual thing that they sprinted down.
It was pretty fucking cool.
And, yeah, it wasn't that many different things that they would do.
But wrestling was one of the big ones, which is really silly in that they're thinking about cutting wrestling from the Olympics.
That's the most ridiculous fucking thing you could do, cut to the wrestling.
It's the original sport in the Olympics.
Why would you cut that?
Besides, it's really technical sport.
And UFC, you know, MMA fighting is really popular now.
And wrestling is basically like half of what that is.
You know, it's half boxing and karate and all that stuff and half wrestling, you know.
So it's the most, it's the oldest sport, wrestling.
So, you know, it's, but, and the women would, they only, they were really sexist in those days.
The only sport that the women would do was sprinting.
And they would run topless.
And, but they didn't participate in any of the other ones, which is too bad, man.
Imagine watching like chicks wrestling, um, nude wrestling.
That'd be badass.
Or like, or like weight category, weight, weight handicapped wrestling, male versus female, like nude oil wrestling.
Or you'd get the 50, the women who's 50 pounds heavier than the guy and then, and then have them go at it.
And that'd be, that, that's badass.
Um, but, uh, but you know, they were pretty creative.
So they had the Olympics, but they also had other games too.
They had the, um, the Delphi, the Delphic games from Delphi where the oracles were.
Dude, Delphi is probably the coolest.
Well, I'd say Machu Picchu, but Delphi is the second coolest archaeological site I've ever been to in my life.
Because it's right up on the side of a cliff of like, not a cliff, but like a really steep slope, like right at the base of these really big cliffs.
And it's this whole city built up alongside it.
And at the very top, they have, they have this big track right at the bottom of the sheer cliff where they would run around and do the sprints and all that kind of stuff.
And, and then they had another, the Achaean games, which was, I think in Athens, but the Olympics were the ones that were the oldest and the biggest.
And they lasted for like 1600 years or something from like 1300 BC all the way until like 350 AD when the Christians, um, outlawed it.
Cause they thought it, they took, they took that whole humanitarianism too far.
They thought it was like, it was a distraction from, uh, being a pious follower of God and stuff.
Because, you know, it was just a backlash from all the barbarism from the gladiatorial combat and stuff and took it too far.
So they banned the Olympics and that was it until 1896 when they had the first Olympics and, and like 1700 years and, um, in Athens.
And then they had it again in Athens in 2004.
But, uh, so that's basically, and I think in Athens, you know, in the original Olympics, they had lots of sports, but not nearly as many as they have now.
But, um, they had, uh, you know, wrestling and, and, uh, not chariot racing, but, you know, running and all that kind of stuff.
The ones that I like to watch are the female gymnastics because their asses are just like so tight and round and fucking, they're so like, uh, they can do the double flips and stuff.
And, and, uh, yeah, the stuff they can do is amazing.
And, uh, and the Americans do really well every time in those.
And, and it's cool to watch them, you know, go and just nail it, you know?
And, uh, and, uh, um, that's probably what I like the most.
And then, oh, beach volleyball.
That's a good one there.
Dude, the athleticism in that is, it's amazing.
Like, I remember, I remember like Misty May and Carrie Walsh, dude, they were, they were like diving and like hitting the ball, you know, like horizontal, right?
Like an inch below the sand and then knocking it back to her.
And then she knocked it over.
I was like, holy shite.
Yeah, dude, beach volleyball, man.
And it's like one of the biggest Olympic sports now too.
Um, it's weird.
I always try to, you know, I, I grew up, you know, racing bikes and stuff as a kid.
And I, I, I, I always wanted to watch the bike race, but you know, they never have it on.
I've never seen it.
They like, it's one of the smaller Olympic sports or something.
They never put it on TV.
I remember I saw a little bit of it in the 2008 Olympics.
I managed to see it on TV, but when I, the last Olympics that I saw, I was at the gym, watching it at the gym.
And I didn't see any of that.
I saw the swimming.
Swimming is like boring to watch because it's just people going up and down, back and forth, but along the thing.
But it's interesting.
I mean, I mean, it's a big sport to watch.
Um, and I, and it's cool for us to watch as an American because we do really well on it.
We had Michael Phelps and, uh, I remember seeing Michael Phelps get the silver by like, like that much dude, because he didn't like reach out.
You know, he didn't, the timing, the other guy just got lucky.
Michael Phelps was a hat.
His head was ahead of the other guy, but the other guy just luckily just his, his, his stroke was just perfect.
And yeah, it was kind of a bummer, but, um, but, um, um, Oh, I, I always like to watch the sprinting.

Um, I think the, one of the interesting ones for this, for the running is like the, uh, the hundred yard, uh, hurdles because you can, you know, it's more technical and you can see him jump over and stuff.
And I like watching the girls do the sprints because, you know, they've got the tight, like spandex on and they've got the muscular bodies.
And I like chicks with the muscular bodies and yeah, they're hot.
Holy shit.
They've got the nicest bodies.

Oh yeah.
And basketball.

I watch a world cup every year.

And, and, you know, just by looking at the form of the people running, like guessing who, who's got the best form, who's got the most energy left and who's going to win.
And that's interesting to watch.
Um, and, um, what else wrestling?
Um, I tried to get into the wrestling this last time, but I don't really understand wrestling.

Winter Olympics.

No, I don't look forward to it.
